questions.text: Do all employees use multi-factor authentication for email and critical systems?
questions.remediation: Enable MFA on email, VPN, admin portals, and cloud apps for all users.
questions.why_it_matters: MFA sharply reduces account-takeover risk when passwords are stolen.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.AA / CIS Control 6 / ISO 27001 A.9.4.2

questions.text: Are strong unique passwords required or managed with a password manager?
questions.remediation: Enforce strong unique passwords and deploy a password manager.
questions.why_it_matters: Weak or reused passwords are a common path for compromise.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.AA / CIS Control 5 / ISO 27001 A.9.4.3

questions.text: Are former employees removed from systems promptly after leaving?
questions.remediation: Create an offboarding checklist that disables accounts and revokes access immediately.
questions.why_it_matters: Stale accounts increase the chance of unauthorized access.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.AC / CIS Control 6 / ISO 27001 A.9.2.6

questions.text: Are company laptops and desktops protected with antivirus or EDR tools?
questions.remediation: Install and centrally manage endpoint protection on all company devices.
questions.why_it_matters: Endpoint controls help detect malware, ransomware, and suspicious execution.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.IP / CIS Control 10 / ISO 27001 A.12.2.1

questions.text: Are company devices encrypted in case they are lost or stolen?
questions.remediation: Enable full-disk encryption on laptops and mobile devices.
questions.why_it_matters: Encryption prevents data exposure when devices are lost, stolen, or repurposed.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.DS / CIS Control 3 / ISO 27001 A.10.1.1

questions.text: Are operating systems and business software updated on a regular schedule?
questions.remediation: Establish patching windows and track critical updates to completion.
questions.why_it_matters: Unpatched systems are often exploited through known vulnerabilities.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.IP / CIS Control 7 / ISO 27001 A.12.6.1

questions.text: Do you track which systems are missing critical security patches?
questions.remediation: Maintain an asset and patch compliance inventory with exception tracking.
questions.why_it_matters: You cannot remediate patch gaps reliably without visibility.
questions.framework_map: NIST ID.AM / CIS Control 1, 7 / ISO 27001 A.8.1.1, A.12.6.1

questions.text: Is sensitive business or customer data stored securely?
questions.remediation: Classify sensitive data and secure it using approved storage and access controls.
questions.why_it_matters: Improper storage increases exposure risk and regulatory impact.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.DS / CIS Control 3 / ISO 27001 A.8.2.3

questions.text: Is access to sensitive data limited only to people who need it?
questions.remediation: Apply least-privilege access and review permissions regularly.
questions.why_it_matters: Overbroad access increases both internal misuse and breach blast radius.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.AC / CIS Control 6 / ISO 27001 A.9.2.3

questions.text: Are important files and systems backed up automatically?
questions.remediation: Implement automated backups for critical data and systems.
questions.why_it_matters: Backups are essential for recovery from ransomware, deletion, or outage.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.IP / CIS Control 11 / ISO 27001 A.12.3.1

questions.text: Are backups tested to confirm they can actually be restored?
questions.remediation: Run restore tests on a defined schedule and record results.
questions.why_it_matters: Untested backups often fail during real incidents when recovery matters most.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.IP / CIS Control 11 / ISO 27001 A.12.3.1

questions.text: Do you have a documented plan for responding to a cyber incident?
questions.remediation: Create a concise incident response playbook with roles, contacts, and escalation steps.
questions.why_it_matters: A defined response plan reduces confusion, downtime, and damage during incidents.
questions.framework_map: NIST RS / CIS Control 17 / ISO 27001 A.16.1.1

questions.text: Do employees receive regular cybersecurity awareness training?
questions.remediation: Provide periodic training on phishing, passwords, safe browsing, and reporting.
questions.why_it_matters: Human behavior is a major factor in phishing and credential compromise.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.AT / CIS Control 14 / ISO 27001 A.7.2.2

questions.text: Do employees know how to report phishing or suspicious activity?
questions.remediation: Set a simple reporting channel and teach staff when and how to use it.
questions.why_it_matters: Fast reporting shortens attacker dwell time and improves containment.
questions.framework_map: NIST RS.AN / CIS Control 17 / ISO 27001 A.16.1.2

questions.text: Are admin accounts separate from normal user accounts?
questions.remediation: Use dedicated admin accounts for privileged activity and standard accounts for daily work.
questions.why_it_matters: Separate admin accounts reduce the impact of phishing and routine misuse.
questions.framework_map: NIST PR.AC / CIS Control 5, 6 / ISO 27001 A.9.2.3

questions.text: Do you monitor for suspicious login attempts or unusual account activity?
questions.remediation: Enable alerting for anomalous logins, failed attempts, and impossible travel.
questions.why_it_matters: Identity monitoring helps surface active account abuse early.
questions.framework_map: NIST DE.CM / CIS Control 8 / ISO 27001 A.12.4.1

questions.text: Are third-party tools or vendors reviewed before getting access to business data?
questions.remediation: Establish a lightweight vendor review checklist before granting access.
questions.why_it_matters: Third parties can introduce security and compliance risk into your environment.
questions.framework_map: NIST ID.SC / CIS Control 15 / ISO 27001 A.15.1.1

questions.text: Is there a clearly assigned owner for cybersecurity decisions?
questions.remediation: Assign a named owner for security decisions, escalation, and accountability.
questions.why_it_matters: Ownership is necessary to drive remediation and maintain security practices.
questions.framework_map: NIST ID.GV / CIS Control 17 / ISO 27001 A.6.1.1
